basics.name: Daniele Infante
basics.label: Chemical Engineering
basics.summary: Chemical Engineering Master's student with a hybrid profile bridging process engineering, computational physics, and advanced IT infrastructure. I combine a strong foundation in **physical modeling** (CFD, numerical analysis) with hands-on **operational tech skills**: from training **Machine Learning models** (CNNs) to deploying and managing **self-hosted server architectures** via Proxmox and Docker. Passionate about bringing data-driven automation and software reliability into industrial and engineering challenges.

work.name: NaLUG (Napoli Linux User Group)
work.summary: Engaged in speaking at Linux Day 2024 and contributing to the group's activities.
highlights: Presented on open-source software and technology trends.
responsibilities: Spoke at Linux Day 2024 as a member of NaLUG, sharing insights on machine learning, automation, and open-source infrastructure.
Organized and participated in technical discussions within the group.
achievements: Enhanced community understanding of open-source software and homelab infrastructure through presentations.

work.name: A.R.S. Cooperative Association
work.summary: Provided civil service support and volunteered in various community events.
highlights: Assisted in organizing and participating in community service projects.
responsibilities: Supported civil service operations and provided assistance during events.
Collaborated with other volunteers to ensure smooth event execution.
achievements: Contributed to the success of multiple community service initiatives.

work.name: Olympiads of Culture and Talent
work.summary: Competed in and organized various cultural and talent olympiads.
responsibilities: Participated in mathematics, physics, culture, and talent olympiads.
Assisted in organizing events and promoting participation among members.
achievements: Gained valuable experience in event organization and competition management.

courses: Machine Learning for Product and Process Engineering

skills.name: Strong resilience and discipline (8 years Martial Arts)

projects.name: Thesis: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) in Fluid Dynamics
projects.description: Research and implementation of Physics-Informed Neural Networks to solve complex fluid dynamics problems by embedding physical laws into deep learning architectures.
highlights: Bridging the gap between traditional CFD and Deep Learning for faster and reliable flow predictions.
Implementation using Python and TensorFlow/PyTorch to satisfy Navier-Stokes equations within the neural network loss function.

projects.name: Computational Engineering & CFD
projects.description: Hands-on experience with OpenFOAM for Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations and physical modeling.
highlights: Proficient in managing complex geometries and mesh generation using Gmsh.
Integrating numerical analysis into process evaluation for industrial applications.

projects.name: Machine Learning & Data Analysis
projects.description: Developed foundational and advanced models in Python for data pattern recognition and predictive analysis.
highlights: Advanced data visualization and statistical analysis of process data.

projects.name: Advanced IT Infrastructure & Automation (Homelab)
projects.description: Deployed and currently manage a private server environment using Proxmox on bare-metal (Ryzen 5), orchestrating various services via Docker instances.
highlights: Developed automated workflows using n8n, Python, and Bash scripting to monitor self-hosted services.
Implemented RSS feed handlers and centralized system notifications through a custom-built Discord hub.
